External Agent: 
Here’s our comprehensive recommendation based on the financing details and additional service requirements:

1. First-Time Buyer Solution Details:
   • Typically, this option offers a 90% Loan-To-Value with lower arrangement fees (CAD 800). Interest rates for first-time buyers in our market usually range between 2.5% to 3.5%, depending on current economic conditions and credit history. Standard loan durations range from 20 to 30 years. In addition to the arrangement fee, the lender may charge minor processing fees and rating adjustments based on credit assessments.
   • Given that this option is tailored for buyers entering the property market, it can be especially beneficial if both parties are involved, as joint financing often allows lenders to consider combined credit profiles and incomes, potentially leading to better terms. That said, stable and well-documented income streams for both parties are crucial.

2. Joint Financing Considerations:
   • When both parties are on the mortgage, it’s important that each contributor’s income is clearly documented. Lenders will evaluate the combined Debt-to-Income ratio along with the reliability of both income sources. This may also require providing detailed documentation of all income streams to secure the preferable first-time buyer terms.
   • For a joint purchase, ensuring all supporting documents are in place—including pay slips, tax returns, and any supplementary earnings records—is key to not only qualify for the mortgage but also negotiate better rates.

3. Ancillary Services – Preliminary Cost Estimates:
   • Legal Services (including property surveys):
       - Legal Package: A Premium Legal Package typically runs at around CAD 1200.
       - Property Surveys: A Full Structural Survey or a Specialist Survey are advisable, costing approximately CAD 650 or CAD 450 respectively.
       - Thus, the combined range could be in the ballpark of CAD 1650 to CAD 1850 total.
   • Moving Services:
       - For a condo move, our Standard Moving option is generally recommended which is around CAD 650.
   • Basic Utility Setup:
       - Basic Utilities setup (gas, electricity, water) has a typical fee of about CAD 120.
       - If you opt for enhanced packages like Premium Utilities (adding internet and phone connections), it could be around CAD 280.

Before finalizing our recommendations, I want to ensure that our financial modeling is as accurate as possible. While you’ve shared an annual side business income figure of CAD 10,000, could you please confirm if this income is consistent through the year or if there are any particular months or seasonal trends that contribute more significantly? Any additional details you could provide regarding the frequency or breakdown of this income would help us in optimizing the financing strategy for the joint purchase.
